pattern: Walking a number's bits from the least significant one and tracking the bit's position parity turns the count directly into a running tally.
hints: Look at n one bit at a time, starting from the least significant bit, which sits at index 0 (an even index).
Keep a running index that increases by one for every bit you look at, and check whether a set bit landed on an even or odd index.
Shift n right one bit at a time until nothing is left, tallying set bits into an even-index bucket and an odd-index bucket, then return both counts in that order.

edge_inputs.description: smallest power of two, single even-index bit
all ones below the byte boundary, alternating even/odd positions
a single high odd-index bit with nothing else set

stress.description: random n across the whole small domain
